projects.title: Interdisciplinary Project: Making of a Global World / Lifelines of National Economy (Class X)
projects.source_text: History Chapter III, Geography Chapter 7, Economics Chapter 4. Internally assessed for 5 marks.

marks_distribution: Class IX: History (20), Geography (20), Political Science (20), Economics (20). Internal Assessment: Periodic Test (5), Multiple Assessment/Project (5), Subject Enrichment Activity (5), Portfolio (5). Total 80+20=100.

Class X Question Paper Design: History (18+2 map pointing, 25%), Political Science (20, 25%), Geography (17+3 map pointing, 25%), Economics (20, 25%). Total Theory Marks: 80. Internal Assessment: Periodic Test (5), Multiple Assessment/Project (5), Subject Enrichment Activity (5), Portfolio (5). Total Internal: 20.
excluded_or_non_assessed_content: Chapter 1 The Story of Village Palampur is assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only.
Pastoralists in the Modern World is assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only.
The Age of Industrialisation is assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only.
Natural Vegetation and Wildlife map pointing (Class IX) - Only map pointing to be evaluated in annual examination.
Lifelines of National Economy map pointing (Class X) - Only map pointing to be evaluated in Board Examination.
teacher_notes: Interdisciplinary projects must be carried out at school between April and September. Carryover of project to home is strictly avoided.
Projects should use eco-friendly products without incurring too much expenditure.
Project records (internal assessment) should be kept for three months from the date of declaration of result for verification.
Map work: Maps available on Govt. of India website may be used.
Assessment includes self-assessment, peer assessment, and teacher assessment as per NEP-2020.
